title: RT-qPCR Detection of Process Controls from Wastewater using AB 7500
author: Jacquelina.Woods
materials: Thaw Master Mix reagents in bench top cool block (chilled at 2-8 °C ) or On ice in master mix preparation hood. Keep Enzyme chilled continually; these enzymes are in glycerol and do not need to be thawed.
Briefly centrifuge all reagents 00:00:05, +/- 2 sec in a personal microcentrifuge to bring liquid to the bottom of tube.
Return all reagents to bench top cool block (chilled at 2-8 °C ) or On ice .
Sample is negative (undetermined) for MNV; or

steps.title: Prepare Master Mix for all sample...
steps.instruction: Prepare Master Mix for all sample and control reactions based on the volumes per 25 µl reaction in this table. Composition of mixes are listed here: Reagent Mixes for RT-qPCR Detection of Process Controls from Wastewater Samples (protocols.io) and should be prepared in advance and stored appropriately. Alternatively, Master Mixes can be prepared from individual components as described here: Master Mix Table for MNV-crAss Assay.pdf . A Use a 1:1000 dilution (made in Primer TE) of FAM reference dye in the N1-N2-IC triplex assay. *Amount varies with concentration of IC RNA. The amount of IC RNA template needs to be adjusted based on the prepared stock concentration to report a Cycle threshold (Ct) of 20-25 when no inhibition is present in the reaction (i.e., the negative control reaction). Do not add IC or sample RNA at this step!

steps.title: Vortex reagent tubes for 00:00:03, +/-...
steps.instruction: Vortex reagent tubes for 00:00:03, +/- 1 sec at setting medium high to high (if vortex has settings).

steps.title: Proceed to hood/area or room where...
steps.instruction: Proceed to hood/area or room where the template is added and thaw IC RNA and sample RNA in this hood/area. RNA templates should be added to reaction tubes in a designated area separate from location where master mixes are prepared. A negative (water) and positive PCR control should be added to each reaction set-up.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge IC RNA 00:00:05, +/-...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge IC RNA 00:00:05, +/- 2 sec in a personal microcentrifuge to bring liquid to the bottom of tube.

steps.title: Add appropriate volume of IC RNA ( 0
steps.instruction: Add appropriate volume of IC RNA ( 0.2 µL per reaction) to Master Mix from Step 1.4 in cold block/on ice.

steps.title: Vortex briefly and centrifuge 00:00:05, +/-...
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly and centrifuge 00:00:05, +/- 2 sec in a personal microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Add 22 µL of Master Mix...
steps.instruction: Add 22 µL of Master Mix to each designated reaction tube or sample wells.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge sample RNA 00:00:05, +/-...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge sample RNA 00:00:05, +/- 2 sec in a personal microcentrifuge to bring liquid to the bottom of tube.

steps.title: Add 3 µL of sample RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL of sample RNA template to each of three reaction tubes or wells.

steps.title: Ensure each plate or run has...
steps.instruction: Ensure each plate or run has appropriate controls (positive and negative controls) included. Positive control prepared as described here: Positive Control Material for RT-qPCR of SARS-CoV-2 and Process Controls (protocols.io) .

steps.title: Seal sample plate or strip tubes
steps.instruction: Seal sample plate or strip tubes. Then, briefly spin 00:00:05, +/- 2 sec .

steps.title: Start run on Applied Biosystems 7500 Fast instrument
steps.instruction: Start run on Applied Biosystems 7500 Fast instrument. Assay parameters were optimized using the AB 7500 software versions 2.0-2.3. If other instruments or software versions are used, additional optimization may be needed.

steps.title: Use the following settings for the Experiment Properties
steps.instruction: Use the following settings for the Experiment Properties: "7500Fast (96 wells)" "Quantitation Standard Curve" "TaqMan Reagents" "Standard (~2 hours to complete run)"

steps.title: Identify the appropriate target reporters (MNV-Cy5,...
steps.instruction: Identify the appropriate target reporters (MNV-Cy5, crAss-JOE, IC-TexasRed) and leave all quenchers as "NFQ-MGB".

steps.title: Select appropriate passive reference dye (FAM for the triplex assay)
steps.instruction: Select appropriate passive reference dye (FAM for the triplex assay).

steps.title: Use the following settings for Run Method
steps.instruction: Use the following settings for Run Method: 25 µL reaction volume Holding stage 1: 50 °C for 00:50:00 Holding stage 2: 95 °C for 00:15:00 Cycling stage: 45 cycles of 95 °C for 00:00:15 , 55 °C for 00:00:20 , 62 °C for 00:01:00 Enable data collection on Step 3 of Cycling stage

steps.title: Adjust analysis settings to appropriate thresholds
steps.instruction: Adjust analysis settings to appropriate thresholds. All thresholds should be set at 0.01 . Baseline start cycle should be set at 3 and baseline end cycle should be set at 10.

steps.title: Verify positive and negative calls for...
steps.instruction: Verify positive and negative calls for each reaction using either linear or log amplification plots.

steps.title: Negative RT-qPCR control is positive (Ct...
steps.instruction: Negative RT-qPCR control is positive (Ct value indicated) for any of the expected targets (MNV or crAssphage);

steps.title: Positive RT-qPCR control is negative (undetermined)...
steps.instruction: Positive RT-qPCR control is negative (undetermined) for expected target/s (MNV or crAssphage);

steps.title: IC is negative (undetermined) in the...
steps.instruction: IC is negative (undetermined) in the sample, or the average of the IC Ct values from the sample replicates are greater than 4 Cts than the IC Ct of the negative RT-qPCR control. Sample is invalid and RT-qPCR should be repeated. If still invalid, an additional concentrate from Virus Concentration from Wastewater Using PEG Precipitation and Ultracentrifugation (protocols.io) should be extracted and tested.

steps.title: Negative RT-qPCR control is negative (undetermined) for expected target/s (i
steps.instruction: Negative RT-qPCR control is negative (undetermined) for expected target/s (i.e., MNV and crAssphage);

steps.title: Positive RT-qPCR control is positive (Ct...
steps.instruction: Positive RT-qPCR control is positive (Ct value indicated) for expected target/s (i.e., MNV and crAssphage); and

steps.title: Internal amplification control (IC) is positive...
steps.instruction: Internal amplification control (IC) is positive in all sample reactions with the average IC Ct values for the sample is less than 4 Cts greater than the IC Ct of the negative RT-qPCR control. Sample is valid , proceed to determination of SARS-CoV-2 results as described RT-qPCR Detection of SARS-CoV-2 from Wastewater Using the AB 7500 (protocols.io) .
